validation.approved_by: DELIVER orchestrator (mason worker) — decomposition matches distill Phase 05 scope + ADR-026 §Consequences → Positive → Reconciles model_sql.py with the ibis pipeline + MR-5 row. Single-step phase: behavior-preserving refactor that has `model_sql.py` consume `dataset_sql.build_staging_sql()` via a new dbt-source compiler plugin, eliminating the parallel-compiler divergence hazard. Characterization tests author BEFORE the change per CLAUDE.md brownfield Feathers discipline.

roadmap.scope_note: Phase 05 only (MR-5). Phases 01 + 02 + 03 + 04 already on main (azurite Phase 03 record at roadmap-phase-03.json + wave-decisions-phase-03.md; chisel Phase 04 record at roadmap-phase-04.json). Phase 05 is the final ibis MR; after it lands the feature is ready for `/nw-finalize ibis-as-only-sql-compiler` in a separate session.

phases.name: MR-5 model_sql.py reconciliation (eliminates parallel-compiler hazard)
phases.description: Refactor backend/app/use_cases/project/_dbt/model_sql.py to consume backend/app/models/dataset_sql.build_staging_sql() rather than re-derive the cleaning operations as dbt CTEs. The parallel cleaning-op compiler (with its .replace("'", "''") defenses) goes away; the ibis pipeline at dataset_sql.py becomes the single source of staging-tier SQL truth. dbt-source ({{ source(project, dataset) }}) macros are emitted via an ibis DuckDB compiler subclass mirroring the established IbisDbtRefDuckDBCompiler pattern (ADR-026 MR-2).

steps.name: Refactor model_sql.py to consume dataset_sql.build_staging_sql() via a dbt-source ibis compiler plugin
steps.scenario: MR-5 reconciliation contract: generate_model_sql delegates to the ibis-staging-SQL pipeline; per-operation CTE-emission helpers are deleted; emitted dbt SQL references the upstream raw dataset via a {{ source(project, dataset) }} macro and the staging-tier SQL semantics are preserved (rows materially identical to legacy output when evaluated against the same fixture).
steps.scenario_name: Refactored generate_model_sql consumes the ibis-staging-SQL pipeline and stays evaluation-equivalent to the legacy CTE compiler
criteria: backend/app/use_cases/project/_dbt/model_sql.py:13-207 no longer contains the per-operation CTE-emission helpers (_fill_null_to_sql, _map_values_to_sql, _transform_to_sql, _case_to_sql, _is_numeric, _build_cleaned_cte, _build_alias_select, _get_schema_columns). After the refactor model_sql.py is a thin call into the ibis pipeline + a dbt-source macro renderer.
No `.replace("'", "''")` literal-escape strings remain anywhere in backend/app/use_cases/project/_dbt/model_sql.py — ibis literal escaping is the closure mechanism per DWD-4 / ADR-026.

A new dbt-source compiler plugin (an ibis DuckDBCompiler subclass mirroring IbisDbtRefDuckDBCompiler) emits `{{ source('<project>', '<dataset>') }}` at the source-table position. The plugin lives in backend/app/use_cases/project/_dbt/ alongside ibis_dbt_source.py and is exported via the _dbt package surface.

Characterization tests in backend/tests/use_cases/project/_dbt/test_model_sql.py (or a parameterized sibling file) capture the legacy byte shape FIRST (Feathers: tests pass against the pre-refactor code) and are then evolved to byte-pin the NEW shape (L2 contract-mirroring rewrite per nw-test-refactoring-catalog — pre-existing assertions on the WITH/source/cleaned/filtered CTE layout interrogate the legacy mechanism, not the contract).

Equivalence assertions: for the parameterized fixture set covered by the legacy unit tests (trim / case-all-modes / fill_null-text / fill_null-numeric / fill_null-quoted / map_values-basic / map_values-quoted / filter / alias / combined / disabled / schema-fallback / injection / reserved-word / cleaning-order), the legacy generate_model_sql output and the new generate_model_sql output evaluate to byte-identical row sets when executed against DuckDB on a seeded source table; OR the new tests pin the post-refactor SQL byte shape directly (whichever is feasible). The crafter chooses; the equivalence claim must be observable in tests, not asserted in commit text.
Walking-skeleton + milestone-1 + milestone-2 acceptance scenarios still GREEN (zero customer-visible regression).
Existing staging-tier dbt-export tests (backend/tests/use_cases/project/_dbt/) still green.
Backend gate ./tools/test/test.sh --backend passes from the repo root before MQ submission.
Step-ID trailer present on the commit.
steps.implementation_notes: READ FIRST: backend/app/use_cases/project/_dbt/model_sql.py (208 lines — the parallel compiler), backend/app/models/dataset_sql.py (build_staging_sql + build_ibis_table; apply_cleaning_mutations / apply_filter_predicates / apply_alias_renames are the three stages already implemented in ibis), backend/app/use_cases/project/_dbt/ibis_dbt_source.py (the established pattern — IbisDbtRefDuckDBCompiler subclasses ibis.backends.sql.compilers.duckdb.DuckDBCompiler and overrides visit_UnboundTable to emit a dbt macro). Read backend/tests/use_cases/project/_dbt/test_intermediate_dbt_ref_characterization.py for the characterization-test cadence MR-2 used. Inspect the single call site: backend/app/use_cases/project/_dbt/__init__.py:159 (`sql = generate_model_sql(project_name_snake, snake_name, ds)`); the only public surface is `generate_model_sql(project_name_snake, dataset_name_snake, dataset) -> str`. The function's contract is: emit dbt staging SQL referencing the upstream raw data via {{ source(project, dataset) }}. The current implementation builds an explicit CTE chain (source -> cleaned -> filtered -> final) with per-operation helpers; the parallel-compiler hazard is that these helpers DUPLICATE the semantics already encoded in dataset_sql.apply_cleaning_mutations / apply_filter_predicates / apply_alias_renames. DESIGN: 1) add IbisDbtSourceDuckDBCompiler in ibis_dbt_source.py (or a sibling module) — same shape as IbisDbtRefDuckDBCompiler but visit_UnboundTable emits `{{ source('<project_snake>', '<dataset_snake>') }}` instead of `{{ ref('<model>') }}`. The compiler is constructed with the (project_snake, dataset_snake) pair — there is exactly one unbound source table per dbt staging model. 2) Refactor generate_model_sql: build the ibis Table via dataset_sql.build_ibis_table(dataset.name, dataset.schema_config, dataset.transforms, table_name=dataset.name), then render it via the new IbisDbtSourceDuckDBCompiler. The result IS the staging SQL with a `{{ source(...) }}` macro at the FROM position. 3) Delete _fill_null_to_sql / _map_values_to_sql / _transform_to_sql / _case_to_sql / _is_numeric / _build_cleaned_cte / _build_alias_select / _get_schema_columns. The whole `import re`, `quote_ident` import, and the dependency on Transform's `condition_sql` attribute also go away (the ibis pipeline reads condition_json instead). FILTER COMPATIBILITY CHECK: dataset_sql.apply_filter_predicates uses `t.condition_json.as_ibis_filter(table)`, but the legacy model_sql.py used `t.condition_sql` directly. Production transforms going through the dbt-export path MUST have condition_json populated. Verify by reading backend/app/models/transform.py (the Transform domain object) — the condition_json field is the ibis path that MR-1/MR-3 established. If a legacy code path can still produce a Transform with only condition_sql and no condition_json, surface that in upstream-issues.md and decide whether to (a) backfill condition_json at the dbt-export composition root or (b) drop those filters with a structured warning in the emitted SQL comment. SCHEMA-FIELDS PASSTHROUGH: the legacy 'no schema fields' fallback (model_sql.py lines 94-98) emits `<expr>, *` — ibis cannot easily emit unbounded `*` after explicit projections; the new behavior MUST handle the no-schema-fields case (either by raising clearly or by emitting the existing dataset_sql `-- Error generating SQL` shape — see dataset_sql.py:48-50 for the established pattern). Decide once, document in the test, hold to it. CHARACTERIZATION TESTS (Feathers brownfield): before changing the production code, add new parameterized tests at backend/tests/use_cases/project/_dbt/test_model_sql_characterization.py (sibling file, separate from test_model_sql.py so the rewrite of the legacy assertions is visible in the diff). Cover: trim / case (upper / lower / title / snake / kebab) / fill_null (text / numeric / quoted) / map_values (basic / quoted) / filter (single / multiple) / alias (basic / spaces-to-snake) / combined (clean+filter+alias) / disabled (each type) / schema-fallback / injection / reserved-word / cleaning-order. Each parameterized case captures the legacy emitted SQL as a fixture string AND records the legacy DuckDB-evaluated row set against a seeded in-memory source table (a small DuckDB connection in the test setup). After the refactor, the same parameterized cases assert the new SQL byte shape (which is the ibis pipeline's output with the dbt-source macro substituted at the FROM clause) AND assert the new DuckDB-evaluated row set matches the legacy fixture. The L2 rewrite of test_model_sql.py's existing assertions (the substring asserts on `WITH source AS`, `cleaned AS`, etc.) is expected — those tests interrogate the legacy CTE mechanism, not the dbt-staging-SQL contract. PROHIBITED (per ADR-026 / DWD-4): no .replace("'", "''") defenses, no sqlglot post-hoc AST validation, no new post-render regex on the emitted SQL. The ibis literal escaping that already covers dataset_sql.py is the closure mechanism for any quoted literals appearing in cleaning operations (e.g., fill_null with a value containing apostrophes, map_values with quoted keys). If any test seems to require a string-escape defense, the ibis composition is wrong — surface in upstream-issues.md and escalate per the iron rule (3-attempt budget). DESIGN BOUNDARY: do not modify the dbt-export composition root in __init__.py:159 — the call signature stays generate_model_sql(project_name_snake, dataset_name_snake, dataset). Do not modify backend/app/models/dataset_sql.py's public surface (build_staging_sql, build_display_sql) — those serve the dataset query-preview path and have their own tests. If a new helper in dataset_sql.py is needed (e.g., to share the ibis table construction with model_sql.py), add it as a clearly-scoped internal function. ITERATION TIGHT LOOP: `cd backend && uv run pytest backend/tests/use_cases/project/_dbt/ -x --tb=short` — fast feedback. Then `./tools/test/test.sh --backend` from the repo root before submitting.

exit_criteria_phase_05: Grep `_fill_null_to_sql\|_map_values_to_sql\|_transform_to_sql\|_case_to_sql\|_is_numeric\|_build_cleaned_cte\|_build_alias_select` against backend/app/use_cases/project/_dbt/model_sql.py returns zero matches (helpers deleted).
Grep `.replace("'", "''")` against backend/app/use_cases/project/_dbt/model_sql.py returns zero matches (literal-escape defenses gone).
Walking-skeleton, milestone-1, and milestone-2 acceptance scenarios GREEN (acceptance suite at tests/acceptance/ibis-as-only-sql-compiler/).

Characterization tests at backend/tests/use_cases/project/_dbt/test_model_sql_characterization.py (or equivalent) exist and pass; equivalence assertions (legacy SQL vs new SQL, evaluated against DuckDB on seeded fixtures) demonstrate row-level fidelity for the legacy helper-covered cases.

Reviewer (nw-software-crafter-reviewer) approves: parallel-compiler hazard eliminated; dataset_sql.py is now the unique staging-tier SQL compiler; closure mechanism for any quoted-literal handling is ibis literal escaping (not a .replace() defense or sqlglot AST pass).
manual_review_gate_after_phase_05: MR-5 closes the parallel-compiler hazard. After it lands: all 11 acceptance scenarios GREEN. Mutation testing kill rate >= 80% on ViewIbisCompiler + ReportIbisCompiler — these landed in Phases 01/03; per the Phase 03 wave-decisions DWD-DELIVER-3 the formal mutmut/cosmic-ray setup is deferred to a project-wide setup MR and the substantive unit-test suite serves as the mutation-aware proxy. Document the same deferral in this phase's wave-decisions. Ready for `/nw-finalize ibis-as-only-sql-compiler` (separate session) per ADR-026 §Consequences → Positive → Reconciles model_sql.py with the ibis pipeline + roadmap §manual_review_gates after_phase 05.
